functions.name: save_free_certs
functions.signature: static int save_free_certs(STACK_OF(X509) *certs, const char *file, const char *desc)
functions.reason: Saves certs to file then frees the entire stack; ends lifetime of the certs container, not just a simple free wrapper.

cfr.name: save_free_certs
cfr.signature: static int save_free_certs(STACK_OF(X509) *certs, const char *file, const char *desc)
cfr.documentation: If file != NULL writes out a stack of certs to the given file. If certs is NULL, the file is emptied. Frees the certs if present. Depending on options use either PEM or DER format, where DER does not make much sense for writing more than one cert! Returns number of written certificates on success, -1 on error.
cfr.id: openssl|apps/cmp.c|save_free_certs|static int save_free_certs(STACK_OF(X509) *certs, const char *file, const char *desc)

functions.reason: Allocates multiple credential objects (keys, certs, CRLs) into caller-owned output pointers; documentation confirms caller responsibility for the allocated objects.

cfr.name: load_key_certs_crls
cfr.signature: int load_key_certs_crls(const char *uri, int format, int maybe_stdin, const char *pass, const char *desc, int quiet, EVP_PKEY **ppkey, EVP_PKEY **ppubkey, EVP_PKEY **pparams, X509 **pcert, STACK_OF(X509) **pcerts, X509_CRL **pcrl, STACK_OF(X509_CRL) **pcrls, EVP_SKEY **pskey)
cfr.documentation: If pcerts is non-NULL and *pcerts == NULL then a new cert list is allocated. If pcerts is non-NULL then all available certificates are appended to *pcerts except any certificate assigned to *pcert. If pcrls is non-NULL and *pcrls == NULL then a new list of CRLs is allocated. If pcrls is non-NULL then all available CRLs are appended to *pcrls except any CRL assigned to *pcrl. On error, any contents of non-NULL credential pointers are freed.
cfr.id: openssl|apps/lib/apps.c|load_key_certs_crls|int load_key_certs_crls(const char *uri, int format, int maybe_stdin, const char *pass, const char *desc, int quiet, EVP_PKEY **ppkey, EVP_PKEY **ppubkey, EVP_PKEY **pparams, X509 **pcert, STACK_OF(X509) **pcerts, X509_CRL **pcrl, STACK_OF(X509_CRL) **pcrls, EVP_SKEY **pskey)

cfr.name: bio_to_mem
cfr.signature: int bio_to_mem(unsigned char **out, size_t *outlen, size_t maxlen, BIO *in)
cfr.documentation: Read whole contents of a BIO into an allocated memory buffer. The return value is one on success, zero on error. If `maxlen` is non-zero, at most `maxlen` bytes are returned, or else, if the input is longer than `maxlen`, an error is returned. If `maxlen` is zero, the limit is effectively `SIZE_MAX`.

functions.name: bn_compute_wNAF
functions.signature: signed char *bn_compute_wNAF(const BIGNUM *scalar, int w, size_t *ret_len)
functions.reason: Allocates and returns a new wNAF array that the caller must free

cfr.name: bn_compute_wNAF
cfr.signature: signed char *bn_compute_wNAF(const BIGNUM *scalar, int w, size_t *ret_len)
cfr.documentation: Determine the modified width-(w+1) Non-Adjacent Form (wNAF) of 'scalar'. This is an array r[] of values that are either zero or odd with an absolute value less than 2^w satisfying scalar = \sum_j r[j]*2^j where at most one of any w+1 consecutive digits is non-zero with the exception that the most significant digit may be only w-1 zeros away from that next non-zero digit.

cfr.documentation: - Perform the generic handling of certificate responses for IR/CR/KUR/P10CR. |rid| must be OSSL_CMP_CERTREQID_NONE if not available, namely for p10cr Returns -1 on receiving pollRep if sleep == 0, setting the checkAfter value. functions.name: dsa_mod_inverse_fermat
functions.signature: static BIGNUM *dsa_mod_inverse_fermat(const BIGNUM *k, const BIGNUM *q, BN_CTX *ctx)
functions.reason: Documentation explicitly states a newly allocated BIGNUM is returned which the caller must free.

cfr.name: dsa_mod_inverse_fermat
cfr.signature: static BIGNUM *dsa_mod_inverse_fermat(const BIGNUM *k, const BIGNUM *q, BN_CTX *ctx)
cfr.documentation: Compute the inverse of k modulo q. Since q is prime, Fermat's Little Theorem applies, which reduces this to mod-exp operation. Both the exponent and modulus are public information so a mod-exp that doesn't leak the base is sufficient. A newly allocated BIGNUM is returned which the caller must free.

cfr.name: ossl_crypto_cleanup_all_ex_data_int
cfr.documentation: Release all "ex_data" state to prevent memory leaks. This can't be made thread-safe without overhauling a lot of stuff, and shouldn't really be called under potential race-conditions anyway (it's for program shutdown after all).

cfr.documentation: Initialise a new CRYPTO_EX_DATA for use in a particular class - including calling new() callbacks for each index in the class used by this variable Thread-safe by copying a class's array of "EX_CALLBACK" entries in the lock, then using them outside the lock. Note this only applies to the global "ex_data" state (ie. class definitions), not 'ad' itself.

cfr.name: msg_encode
cfr.signature: static uint8_t *msg_encode(const uint8_t *msg, size_t msg_len, const uint8_t *ctx, size_t ctx_len, int encode, uint8_t *tmp, size_t tmp_len, size_t *out_len)
cfr.documentation: @param tmp A small buffer that may be used if the message is small. @param tmp_len The size of |tmp| @param out_len The size of the returned encoded buffer. @returns A buffer containing the encoded message. If the passed in |tmp| buffer is big enough to hold the encoded message then it returns |tmp| otherwise it allocates memory which must be freed by the caller. If |encode| is 0 then it returns |msg|. NULL is returned if there is a failure.

On successful exit *verifier will point to a newly allocated BIGNUM containing the verifier and (if a salt was not provided) *salt will be populated with a newly allocated BIGNUM containing a random salt. cfr.name: i2d_X509_AUX
cfr.documentation: Serialize trusted certificate to *pp, or just return the required buffer length if pp == NULL. When pp is not NULL, but *pp == NULL, we allocate the buffer, but since we're writing two ASN.1 objects back to back, we can't have i2d_X509() do the allocation, nor can we allow i2d_X509_CERT_AUX() to increment the allocated buffer.

functions.name: PACKET_memdup
functions.signature: int PACKET_memdup(const PACKET *pkt, unsigned char **data, size_t *len)
functions.reason: Allocates a new buffer via OPENSSL_memdup and stores it in *data; also frees old *data if non-NULL (realloc pattern), but primary purpose is producing owned allocation

cfr.name: PACKET_memdup
cfr.signature: int PACKET_memdup(const PACKET *pkt, unsigned char **data, size_t *len)
cfr.documentation: Copy |pkt| bytes to a newly allocated buffer and store a pointer to the result in |*data|, and the length in |len|. If |*data| is not NULL, the old data is OPENSSL_free'd. If the packet is empty, or malloc fails, |*data| will be set to NULL. Returns 1 if the malloc succeeds and 0 otherwise. Does not forward PACKET position (because it is typically the last thing done with a given PACKET).

functions.reason: Creates a new stack when *a is NULL and transfers ownership to caller; caller must free with SCT_LIST_free

cfr.name: o2i_SCT_LIST
cfr.signature: STACK_OF(SCT) *o2i_SCT_LIST(STACK_OF(SCT) **a, const unsigned char **pp, size_t len)
cfr.documentation: If "a" or "*a" is NULL, a new stack will be created that the caller is responsible for freeing (by calling SCT_LIST_free). "**pp" and "*pp" must not be NULL. Upon success, "*pp" will point to after the last bytes read, and a stack will be returned. Upon failure, a NULL pointer will be returned, and the position of "*pp" is not defined.

cfr.documentation: If "a" or "*a" is NULL, a new stack will be created that the caller is responsible for freeing (by calling SCT_LIST_free). "**pp" and "*pp" must not be NULL. Upon success, "*pp" will point to after the last bytes read, and a stack will be returned. Upon failure, a NULL pointer will be returned, and the position of "*pp" is not defined.

functions.name: CTLOG_new_ex
functions.signature: CTLOG *CTLOG_new_ex(EVP_PKEY *public_key, const char *name, OSSL_LIB_CTX *libctx, const char *propq)
functions.reason: Creates and returns a new CTLOG instance; caller owns it and must free with CTLOG_free

cfr.name: CTLOG_new_ex
cfr.signature: CTLOG *CTLOG_new_ex(EVP_PKEY *public_key, const char *name, OSSL_LIB_CTX *libctx, const char *propq)
cfr.documentation: Creates a new CT log instance with the given |public_key| and |name| and associates it with the give library context |libctx| and property query string |propq|. Takes ownership of |public_key| but copies |name|. Returns NULL if malloc fails or if |public_key| cannot be converted to DER. Should be deleted by the caller using CTLOG_free when no longer needed.

cfr.name: CTLOG_new_from_base64_ex
cfr.signature: int CTLOG_new_from_base64_ex(CTLOG **ct_log, const char *pkey_base64, const char *name, OSSL_LIB_CTX *libctx, const char *propq)
cfr.documentation: Creates a new CTLOG instance with the base64-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo DER in |pkey_base64| and associated with the given library context |libctx| and property query string |propq|. The |name| is a string to help users identify this log. Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure. Should be deleted by the caller using CTLOG_free when no longer needed.

functions.name: tls_int_free

functions.name: ossl_tls_buffer_release
functions.reason: Releases the internal buffer allocation within a TLS_BUFFER struct via OPENSSL_free; does not free the struct itself.

functions.name: TLS_RL_RECORD_release

functions.name: tls_release_write_buffer_int

functions.name: tls_release_write_buffer
functions.reason: Wrapper that releases all write buffers of a record layer by calling tls_release_write_buffer_int.

functions.name: tls_release_read_buffer
functions.reason: Frees the read buffer allocation within an existing record layer; object itself is not freed.

functions.name: tls_release_record
functions.reason: Releases a previously consumed record's data back to the layer, potentially freeing the read buffer; operates on a record handle, not the whole object.

functions.signature: static int ct_move_scts(STACK_OF(SCT) **dst, STACK_OF(SCT) *src, sct_source_t origin)
functions.reason: May create a new SCT stack at *dst and transfers ownership of SCTs there; net effect is producing an owned output at *dst.

cfr.name: ct_move_scts
cfr.signature: static int ct_move_scts(STACK_OF(SCT) **dst, STACK_OF(SCT) *src, sct_source_t origin)
cfr.documentation: Moves SCTs from the |src| stack to the |dst| stack. The source of each SCT will be set to |origin|. If |dst| points to a NULL pointer, a new stack will be created and owned by the caller. Returns the number of SCTs moved, or a negative integer if an error occurs. The |dst| stack is created and possibly partially populated even in case of error, likewise the |src| stack may be left in an intermediate state.

cfr.documentation: names separated by the group or the tuple delimiter, with per-group prefixes to (1) add a key share for this group, (2) ignore the group if unknown to the current context, (3) delete a previous occurrence of the group in the current tuple. cfr.name: create_ctx
cfr.documentation: Usage: - Call this function to create an SSL_CTX before starting the QUIC server. - Ensure valid paths for the certificate and private key are provided. Note: - The ALPN callback only supports the predefined protocol defined in `alpn_ossltest`.

cfr.name: create_socket
cfr.documentation: - Call this function to set up a socket for handling incoming QUIC connections. Notes: - This function assumes UDP (`SOCK_DGRAM`). - This function accepts on both IPv4 and IPv6. - The specified port is converted to network byte order using `htons`.
